steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The passage opens with a claim — ‘We are creatures of the sun’ who ‘love light and life’ — and uses that to explain why people crowd into cities. Everything that follows (loneliness at night, longing for gas-lit streets) supports and extends that opening claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘crowded areas can cause people to feel as though they are children again’: The child simile describes how people feel alone at night, not why cities are crowded. This treats a supporting detail as the main point. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘caution people not to be so quick to dismiss… rural areas’: The passage never tells readers to reconsider rural life; it explains urban migration, not defends the countryside. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘address common traits that motivate many people to choose to live in urban environments’: This matches the passage’s structure — the narrator identifies shared human traits (love of light and life) and links them directly to urban migration. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘most people tend to prefer hillsides in the country to… towns and cities’: The passage says the opposite — people prefer cities, especially at night. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
